center.name: 福莫萨省
center.summary: 福莫萨省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括Lesser yellow-headed vulture、Black-crowned tityra、Olivaceous elaenia等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: inquisitor: east and southeastern Brazil from southern Piauí south through western Bahia and beyond to northern Rio Grande do Sul, eastern Paraguay, and northeastern Argentina to Corrientes Province including eastern Formosa and Chaco provinces The black-crowned tityra inhabits lowland evergreen forest and secondary forest in the tropical and lower subtropical zones. It favors the forest canopy and edges, clearings and areas along waterways

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: certhoides is found in northeastern Argentina from Río Negro Province north to Formosa Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The freckle-breasted thornbird is found in northern Argentina between the provinces of Formosa and Buenos Aires, in Brazil from Paraná state south, and in most of Uruguay. It possibly also occurs in Parguay; the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society has only sight records from that country and so treats it as hypothetical there. The species inhabits a variety of landscapes, almost always near water

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland, swamps, mangroves, and heavily degraded former forest. It may wander over dry fields and clearings. It is not generally found in high-altitude regions. This bird with its somehow crow-like aspect gave foot to the naming of the Quebrada de los Cuervos (Crows Ravine) in Uruguay, where they dwell together with the black vulture and the turkey vulture. File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Campos dos Goytacazes - RJ, Brasil imported from iNaturalist photo 356680714.jpg|Flock, in Rio de Janeiro, Brasil File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Pilcomayo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 478244272.jpg|With a turkey vulture, in Formosa, Argentina File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Bermejo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 475887416.jpg|With a crested caracara, in Formosa, Argentina File:Vultures at Celestún Garbage Dump - Flickr - treegrow.jpg|With a black vulture, Yucatán, Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The olivaceous elaenia is found from southern Goiás and southern Bahia in eastern Brazil south through eastern Paraguay and into eastern Uruguay and northern Argentina's Formosa, Chaco, and Misiones provinces. Its first record in Uruguay did not come until 2000. The olivaceous elaenia inhabits interior, edges, and clearings of humid tropical forest and also gallery forest, secondary forest, and more open woodlands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The firewood-gatherer is found in Brazil from Goiás and Minas Gerais south through Rio Grande do Sul, central and eastern Paraguay, essentially all of Uruguay, and northern Argentina from Formosa and Misiones provinces south to Río Negro Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The azure jay is found in Brazil from southern São Paulo south through Paraná, Santa Catarina, and Rio Grande do Sul almost to Uruguay. Its range continues into the northeastern Argentinian provinces of Formosa, Chaco, Corrientes, and Misiones.{{cite book | last =de la Peña | first =Martín R
